decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch, and Incineroar cannot effectively pressure it. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Drain Punch and forces Iron Hands out, gaining momentum.
I outspeed Iron Hands and can secure an OHKO with Moonblast, which is the highest priority action according to the decision rules.
Flutter Mane is threatened with an OHKO by Flare Blitz and cannot secure a KO this turn; switching to Incineroar provides a safe pivot to resist the incoming Fire-type attack.

Knock Off provides the best damage output and utility by removing the opponent's item, which is crucial in an Incineroar mirror match. Since I am ahead in HP and the opponent's moves are not threatening a quick KO, staying in to chip damage and remove their item is the optimal play.

Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch, and Incineroar cannot KO it back. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Drain Punch and forces the opponent to switch or risk a super-effective hit.
Flutter Mane has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Incineroar provides Intimidate support to soften Iron Hands' physical attacks and can pivot with U-turn or threaten with Knock Off.

Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch and Wild Charge, and Incineroar's damage output is too low to force a trade. Switching to Calyrex-Ice allows me to pivot into a more threatening offensive presence while maintaining momentum.
High Horsepower is a guaranteed 2HKO on Iron Hands and provides the best damage output. Since Iron Hands cannot OHKO Calyrex-Ice, staying in to secure the 2HKO is the optimal play.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and the opponent's most likely play is to stay in or pivot, making this the most aggressive and effective play to force progress against Incineroar.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and forces Incineroar to either switch or risk being knocked out, maintaining offensive pressure while Incineroar is in a range where it cannot safely OHKO Calyrex-Ice.

High Horsepower is a 2HKO on Incineroar and is the most reliable way to remove it before it can pivot out with Parting Shot or threaten with Flare Blitz. Since Calyrex-Ice is at 76% HP, it can survive a Knock Off or a Flare Blitz, allowing it to secure the KO on the following turn.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO on Iron Hands and provides the best damage output. Since Iron Hands is likely to switch or use a weak move, staying in to pressure the opponent is the optimal play.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO on Incineroar and deals significant damage. Since Incineroar is at 47% HP, this move is guaranteed to KO, satisfying the priority to take the KO immediately.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO on Incineroar and deals significant damage. Since Incineroar is at 47% HP, this move is guaranteed to KO, satisfying the priority to take the KO immediately.

High Horsepower is a 2HKO on Iron Hands and provides the most consistent damage output. Since Iron Hands is at 41%, this move is highly likely to secure the KO regardless of whether they stay in or attempt a suboptimal switch.

Iron Hands is at 17% HP, meaning Glacial Lance will easily secure the KO regardless of whether they stay in or switch, as it is a guaranteed 2HKO on full health and will certainly finish off a weakened target.

Calyrex-Ice is at 14% and will be KO'd by almost any move from Incineroar, including Knock Off. Switching to Incineroar allows me to safely pivot, neutralize the opponent's attack with Intimidate, and maintain momentum.

Since this is a speed tie and the opponent is likely to use Parting Shot or Knock Off, using Parting Shot allows me to pivot safely, reduce their offensive stats, and bring in a healthier teammate to maintain momentum.
Since both Incineroars are speed-tied and trading neutral damage, Knock Off is the most effective way to remove the opponent's item and gain long-term utility while maintaining momentum.

Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Incineroar is the best pivot to handle the opponent's Incineroar, as it can safely switch in, threaten an Intimidate, and potentially use Parting Shot to gain momentum.
The mirror match is neutral and low-impact; using Parting Shot allows me to lower the opponent's stats and pivot into a more favorable matchup while maintaining momentum.
Knock Off provides the most consistent chip damage and utility to remove the opponent's item, and since we are in a speed tie, attacking is safer than pivoting to maintain momentum.
Knock Off provides the best damage output to pressure the opponent's Incineroar, which is already at 50% HP, while avoiding the chip damage of Flare Blitz recoil.
Knock Off is the most reliable way to secure a KO on the weakened Incineroar while removing its item, and it avoids the risk of a speed tie with Parting Shot or Fake Out.
The opponent's Incineroar is at 4% HP, making it a guaranteed KO with Knock Off. This removes the opponent's pivot and forces them to bring in a fresh Pokemon.
